Tomato.diseaseOverview: Early blight is a common fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani. It causes dark concentric lesions on older leaves, leading to defoliation and reduced yield.
Tomato.whyThisPrediction: Typical symptoms include target-board like dark spots with yellow halos on lower/older leaves.
chemicalTreatments: Apply Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/litre of water every 7-10 days.
Use Chlorothalonil 75% WP at 2g/litre as a preventive spray.
Apply Iprodione 50% WP at 1g/litre for curative treatment.
organicTreatments: Spray Neem oil (3ml/litre) + Bordeaux mixture (1%) every 10 days.
Apply Trichoderma viride bio-fungicide as a soil drench.
Use baking soda solution (1 tsp per litre) as a foliar spray.
preventionTips: Remove and destroy infected plant debris after harvest.
Maintain wide spacing between plants for good air circulation.
Avoid overhead irrigation; use drip irrigation.
Rotate crops — avoid planting tomato/potato in the same field for 2 years.
Use certified disease-resistant seeds.

Tomato.diseaseOverview: Late blight, caused by Phytophthora infestans, is a destructive disease affecting both foliage and fruit. It spreads rapidly in cool, moist conditions.
Tomato.whyThisPrediction: Water-soaked, greyish-green lesions on leaves that turn brown-black with white mold on the undersides are classic signs.
chemicalTreatments: Apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil Gold) at 2.5g/litre every 7 days.
Spray Cymoxanil + Mancozeb at 2g/litre.
Use Dimethomorph 50% WP at 1g/litre for systemic control.
organicTreatments: Apply Copper oxychloride (3g/litre) as a protective spray.
Use Bordeaux mixture (1%) every 7-10 days.
Spray potassium bicarbonate solution (5g/litre) weekly.
preventionTips: Avoid planting in low-lying, poorly-drained areas.
Ensure proper spacing and staking for ventilation.
Scout fields regularly especially during cool, humid weather.
Destroy volunteer tomato and potato plants.
Use resistant varieties like 'Pusa Hybrid' or 'Arka Rakshak'.

Tomato.diseaseOverview: Leaf mold (Passalora fulva) causes pale greenish-yellow patches on upper leaf surfaces and olive-green to grayish-purple mold on the undersides.
Tomato.whyThisPrediction: Olive-green or grayish-brown velvety growth on the lower leaf surface with yellow patches above are hallmarks of this disease.
chemicalTreatments: Apply Chlorothalonil 75% WP at 2g/litre every 10 days.
Spray Difenoconazole 25% EC at 0.5ml/litre.
Use Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/litre as protective spray.
organicTreatments: Spray diluted neem oil (5ml/litre) every 7 days.
Apply baking soda + soap solution (1 tsp/litre + few drops soap).
Improve ventilation to reduce humidity around plants.
preventionTips: Maintain greenhouse humidity below 85%.
Remove and destroy infected leaves immediately.
Avoid wetting foliage during irrigation.
Plant resistant varieties such as 'Stirling' or 'Ferline'.
Disinfect tools and equipment regularly.

Tomato.diseaseOverview: Your tomato crop appears healthy. No disease symptoms detected.
Tomato.whyThisPrediction: Leaves show uniform green color with no spots, lesions, or discoloration.
preventionTips: Continue regular scouting every 3-5 days.
Maintain balanced fertilization (NPK).
Ensure consistent watering to prevent blossom end rot.
Apply preventive neem oil spray every 14 days.
Keep field free of weeds to reduce pest pressure.

Potato.diseaseOverview: Caused by Alternaria solani, potato early blight produces dark, concentric ring-patterned lesions on older leaves, reducing photosynthesis and tuber yield.
Potato.whyThisPrediction: Dark brown, circular to oval spots with concentric rings (target-board pattern) on lower/mature leaves are the key symptom.
chemicalTreatments: Spray Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/litre at 10-day intervals.
Apply Azoxystrobin 23% SC at 1ml/litre for systemic control.
Use Tebuconazole 25.9% EC at 1ml/litre for curative treatment.
organicTreatments: Apply Bordeaux mixture (1%) every 10-14 days.
Spray Trichoderma harzianum bio-fungicide at 5g/litre.
Use neem-based formulations (Azadirachtin 0.03%) every 7 days.
preventionTips: Use certified, disease-free seed tubers.
Maintain proper plant spacing (45cm x 20cm).
Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization.
Practice 3-year crop rotation with non-solanaceous crops.
Destroy crop debris after harvest by deep plowing.

Potato.diseaseOverview: The most devastating potato disease, caused by Phytophthora infestans. Under favorable conditions, it can destroy an entire crop within 7-10 days.
Potato.whyThisPrediction: Water-soaked, rapidly expanding lesions on leaves with white sporulation on leaf undersides during humid conditions are diagnostic.
chemicalTreatments: Apply Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP at 2.5g/litre every 7 days.
Spray Cymoxanil 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP at 3g/litre.
Use Fenamidone 10% + Mancozeb 50% WG at 3g/litre.
organicTreatments: Apply Copper hydroxide at 3g/litre as a preventive measure.
Use Bordeaux mixture (1%) weekly during high-risk periods.
Spray potassium phosphonate (5ml/litre) for resistance induction.
preventionTips: Plant certified late-blight-resistant varieties (e.g., Kufri Jyoti).
Avoid planting near tomato fields.
Hill up plants to protect tubers from infection.
Monitor weather forecasts — spray before rain/fog spells.
Destroy all infected plant material; do not compost.

Potato.diseaseOverview: Your potato crop appears healthy. No disease symptoms detected.
Potato.whyThisPrediction: Foliage shows normal dark green color with no spots, lesions, or wilting.
preventionTips: Continue hilling up soil around plants for tuber protection.
Maintain regular irrigation, especially during tuber initiation.
Scout for aphids (late blight virus vectors) regularly.
Apply balanced fertilizer as per soil test recommendations.
Keep field edges free of weeds and volunteer plants.

Corn.diseaseOverview: Common rust (Puccinia sorghi) produces cinnamon-brown pustules scattered on both leaf surfaces. Severe infections reduce photosynthesis and grain fill.
Corn.whyThisPrediction: Small, circular to elongated cinnamon-brown powdery pustules on upper and lower leaf surfaces are the classic symptom.
chemicalTreatments: Apply Propiconazole 25% EC at 1ml/litre at first symptom appearance.
Spray Tebuconazole 25.9% EC at 0.5ml/litre every 14 days.
Use Azoxystrobin 23% SC at 1ml/litre for systemic control.
organicTreatments: Apply sulfur dust (20-30 kg/ha) during early infection.
Spray neem oil (5ml/litre) + copper soap every 10 days.
Use Bacillus subtilis biofungicide as a foliar spray.
preventionTips: Plant resistant hybrids — check local recommended varieties.
Avoid late planting which coincides with humid conditions.
Maintain adequate plant spacing for airflow.
Monitor regularly during periods of high humidity.
Avoid excessive nitrogen that promotes lush foliage.

Corn.diseaseOverview: Caused by Exserohilum turcicum, Northern Leaf Blight produces long cigar-shaped lesions that can coalesce and kill large portions of leaves.
Corn.whyThisPrediction: Long (5-15cm), elliptical, tan/grey-green cigar-shaped lesions on leaves are the hallmark symptom.
chemicalTreatments: Spray Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/litre preventively from tasselling.
Apply Propiconazole 25% EC at 1ml/litre at disease onset.
Use Carbendazim 50% WP at 1g/litre as a curative spray.
organicTreatments: Apply Trichoderma viride bio-fungicide at 5g/litre as a foliar spray.
Use neem kernel extract (5%) spray every 7-10 days.
Apply Bordeaux mixture (0.5%) at early stages.
preventionTips: Use resistant hybrids with Ht1 or Ht2 genes.
Rotate with non-host crops like legumes or wheat.
Plow under infected debris after harvest.
Avoid dense planting — follow recommended spacing.
Ensure balanced crop nutrition, especially potassium.

Corn.diseaseOverview: Your corn crop appears healthy. No disease symptoms detected.
Corn.whyThisPrediction: Leaves are uniformly green with no lesions, pustules, or abnormal discoloration.
preventionTips: Monitor for fall armyworm, especially at whorl stage.
Ensure adequate zinc and boron nutrition for healthy growth.
Scout fields twice a week during vegetative stages.
Maintain proper irrigation during tasselling and grain fill.
Weed control is critical in the first 30 days after sowing.

Wheat.diseaseOverview: Yellow rust (stripe rust), caused by Puccinia striiformis, is one of the most damaging wheat diseases in cooler regions. It can cause yield losses up to 70%.
Wheat.whyThisPrediction: Bright yellow-orange pustules arranged in stripes along leaf veins, giving a striped appearance, are the defining symptom.
chemicalTreatments: Apply Propiconazole 25% EC at 1ml/litre at first appearance.
Spray Tebuconazole 250 EW at 1ml/litre every 14 days.
Use Trifloxystrobin + Tebuconazole (Nativo) at 0.5g/litre.
organicTreatments: Apply sulfur-based fungicides (Sulfex 80% WG) at 3g/litre.
Spray potassium silicate solution to enhance plant resistance.
Use garlic extract spray (50g/litre) as a preventive measure.
preventionTips: Grow resistant varieties such as HD 2967, GW 496, or WH 1105.
Sow at recommended time — delayed sowing increases rust risk.
Monitor fields from tillering stage onwards.
Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization.
Scout neighboring fields as spores travel on wind.

Wheat.diseaseOverview: Powdery mildew (Blumeria graminis) appears as white powdery patches on leaves and stems. It thrives in cool, humid conditions and reduces grain quality.
Wheat.whyThisPrediction: White to grey powdery fungal colonies on upper leaf surfaces, sheaths, and stems are the primary symptom.
chemicalTreatments: Spray Triadimefon 25% WP at 1g/litre at first symptom.
Apply Propiconazole 25% EC at 1ml/litre every 14 days.
Use Kresoxim-methyl 44.3% SC at 0.5ml/litre for systemic action.
organicTreatments: Apply sulfur dust (20kg/ha) at early infection stage.
Spray diluted milk (1:9 ratio) — fatty acids inhibit spore germination.
Use potassium bicarbonate spray (5g/litre) every 7 days.
preventionTips: Use resistant varieties suited to your region.
Avoid dense planting — follow spacing of 20-22.5cm row spacing.
Balanced fertilization — avoid excess nitrogen.
Timely sowing reduces risk as crop escapes favorable conditions.
Remove and destroy heavily infected plant material.

Wheat.diseaseOverview: Your wheat crop appears healthy. No disease symptoms detected.
Wheat.whyThisPrediction: Crop shows uniform dark green leaves, erect tillers, and no visible pustules, spots, or mold growth.
preventionTips: Apply second dose of nitrogen fertilizer at tillering stage.
Maintain recommended plant population density.
Scout for aphids and yellow dwarf virus weekly.
Ensure adequate irrigation at crown root initiation and heading.
Keep field edges weeded to reduce rust inoculum.

Rice.diseaseOverview: Rice blast (Magnaporthe oryzae) is the most devastating rice disease worldwide. It can affect leaves, collar, nodes, and panicles at all growth stages.
Rice.whyThisPrediction: Diamond/spindle-shaped lesions with grey/white centers and brown borders on leaves, or blackening of the neck (neck rot) are diagnostic.
chemicalTreatments: Spray Tricyclazole 75% WP at 0.6g/litre at first symptom.
Apply Isoprothiolane 40% EC at 1.5ml/litre every 10-14 days.
Use Carbendazim 50% WP at 1g/litre for early infections.
organicTreatments: Apply silicon (sodium silicate, 2g/litre) as a resistance inducer.
Spray Pseudomonas fluorescens bio-agent at 5g/litre.
Use Trichoderma asperellum at 5g/litre as foliar spray.
preventionTips: Use blast-resistant varieties (IR64, Swarna, Pusa Basmati 1).
Avoid excessive nitrogen — split application reduces risk.
Maintain 5cm standing water to reduce leaf wetness.
Do not transplant seedlings older than 30 days.
Seed treatment with Trichoderma before sowing.

Rice.diseaseOverview: Brown spot disease (Bipolaris oryzae) is linked to nutrient-deficient soils. It causes small round brown lesions on leaves and glumes, reducing grain quality.
Rice.whyThisPrediction: Small, circular to oval brown lesions with a light brown center surrounded by a dark brown border on leaves are the hallmark symptom.
chemicalTreatments: Seed treatment with Captan 75% WS at 2.5g/kg seed.
Foliar spray of Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/litre.
Apply Propiconazole 25% EC at 1ml/litre at booting/heading stage.
organicTreatments: Apply potassium fertilizer (MOP at 60kg/ha) to reduce susceptibility.
Spray micronutrient mixture (manganese + zinc) to boost plant immunity.
Use Neem cake soil application at 250kg/ha.
preventionTips: Conduct soil testing and correct nutrient deficiencies.
Ensure adequate potassium and silicon nutrition.
Use clean, certified, disease-free seeds.
Maintain optimum plant population.
Drain and dry field periodically to improve soil aeration.

Rice.diseaseOverview: Your rice crop appears healthy. No disease symptoms detected.
Rice.whyThisPrediction: Crop shows uniform erect tillers with deep green, smooth leaves and no visible lesions or discoloration.
preventionTips: Scout weekly for stem borer and leaf folder pests.
Maintain 2-5cm water level during vegetative stage.
Apply top-dressing nitrogen at active tillering stage.
Pull out and destroy any rogues or off-type plants.
Drain field 10 days before harvest for proper ripening.
